series_description: The contract change form allows individual changes to the contract to take place. (For example: If the owner dies he is replaced by the successor owner. A new successor owner would need to be appointed.) Confidential by Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are kept permanently ; Paper: Retain 1 year on site, image

series_description: Payroll deduction forms allow the customer to automatically withdraw money from his paycheck and deposit it into his account with the Ohio Tuition Trust. Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are kept permanently ; Paper: Image

series_description: The cash receipt record is a book of receipts. These receipts are written and distributed to customers who bring payments to the agency. Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep on-site 1 year for audit

series_description: A form filled out to request funding for a customer, includes the customer's name, address, contract number, social security number and amount to be paid. (Refunds and Electronic Funds Transfer Fees are included in this type of form.) Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic files are kept for the life of the contract ; Paper: Store on site for 1 year until audit, store off-site for 1 year

series_description: Transfer forms allow a customer to transfer funds to a different beneficiary (contract). Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year on-site for audit

series_description: The address change form allows the owner of the contract to change the address of anyone on the account. It can only be submitted in writing, include contract numbers, and signed by the owner. Confidential - Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are kept permanently ; Paper: Image

series_description: General correspondence includes pending contract letters, non-sufficient fund letters, and fee surveys to college. Confidiential - Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are kept permanently ; Paper: Image

series_description: The contract amendment allows the owner of the contract to amend or modify the original contract to allow full ownership to the owner instead of the beneficiary. It is legally binding and considered confidential according to the Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are kept permanently ; Paper: Keep on-site for 1 year, image

series_description: The electronic funds transfer allows the customer to automatically withdraw funds from a bank account to be saved in his College Savings fund. Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are retained permanently ; Paper: Retain records of electronid funds transfers until imaged and image is verified for quality and completeness

series_description: Visitor Log book records names of visitors and times of entry and departure.
retention_statement: Paper: Store on site 1 year

series_description: The deposit slip from the bank and any paper work associated with the check for Electronic Funds make up purchases, volume discount make up purchases, fee payments, Scholarship checks or multiple purchases with 1 check. (Associated paperwork may include: an application, transfer form, coupon or letter.)
retention_statement: Paper: Store on site 1 year until audit, store off-site 1 year

series_description: The Withdrawl Reqest Form allows the owner of the account to withdraw funds to be used for college or as a refund. These forms indicate due dates and amount to be paid to the college, banking information of the account owner and hardship records. Forms would include: Prepaid Tuition Use Forms, Qualified Withdrawal Request Forms, Non-qualified Withdrawal Request Forms, Hardship Withdrawal Requests, Withdrawal Request Forms and all supporting documentation. Confidential - Ohio Revised Code 3334.11
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year on-site for audit

series_description: This form allowed the account owner to convert tuition credits to tuition units during a specific 6 month period of time. The Tuition Credit Coversion Election Form contains the account owners name, social security number, account number, address and signature. This form is considered confidential according to the Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper files will be kept for the life of the contract.

series_description: A report listing all Automatic Contribution Transactions returned for a particular day. Considered Confidential - Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year on-site for audit

series_description: An agreement with the Ohio Tuition Trust Authority signed by an employer setting up payroll deduction for their employees dictating the employer''s method of payment.
retention_statement: Paper: Lifetime of the agency.

series_description: This form verifies the account owner''s: account number, name, address, social security number, driver''s license number and mother''s maiden name. This is the financial identity verification for the Patriot Act. Confidential by Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Image ; Electronic: Image files are kept for the life of the contract.

series_description: The roster provides a list of students in the Ohio Tuition Trust program with individual students names, social security numbers and amount to be paid to different colleges around U.S. Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic records are kept for the life of the contract, usually 25 years.

series_description: A report that lists all cash transactions processed for a particular day. Could Include: Audit reconciliation report, Lockbox report, Bank reconciliation report, EFT summary, Electronic Payroll deduction breakdown, Various Email for adjustments, Reallocation report, Fund manager reports, Nacha file returns, Voucher report and check returns.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year on-site for audit

series_description: Call volume and Representative statistics for a particular day.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year on-site

series_description: Requests for adjustments made to College Advantage Customers Accounts and supporting documentation. Considered confidential - Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 30 years

series_description: The Account information change form allows the owner of an account to update his personal information (name, address, phone number), update the existing beneficiary information (name, date of birth, social security number, address), or update, change or add a successor owner (name, date of birth, social security number, address). This form is considered confidential according to the Ohio Revised Code 3334.11. This form now incorporates the former Address Change form and Successor Change form.
retention_statement: Paper: Image ; Electronic: Imaged files are kept for the life of the contract.

series_description: A reallocation allows the owner of an account to move all or a portion of each fund into a new fund option once per calendar year. Information includes Owners account number, name, ss#, address, DL#, mother''s maiden name, beneficiary name, ss#, address, current fund and new fund and signature of owner. Confidential - Ohio Revised Code 3334.11
retention_statement: Paper: Image ; Electronic: Images are kept for the life of the contract.

series_description: The application is a legally binding agreement between the customer and the Ohio Tuition Trust Authority (OTTA). The application allows a customer to open a 529 account with the Ohio Tuition Trust Authority. It contains personal information for an account owner, beneficiary and successor. The original fund choice and contribution type is designated with the application. Future allocations are optional, but could be included on the application. It is considered a confidential record according to Ohio Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Imaged files are kept for the life of the contract ; Paper: Retain contracts for the life of the contract.

series_description: Contribution slips or vouchers contain information about the customer''s account. The contribution slip is used to verify account information and investment instructions when a payment is made. A voucher comes from a payroll company and includes information used by the Ohio Tuition Trust Authority to identify a customer''s account and deposit instructions. Confidential - Revised Code 3334.11.
retention_statement: Paper: Image ; Electronic: Images are kept for the life of the contract.

series_description: Data feed files received from outside recordkeeping entities that are then loaded and saved on our system.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for a minimum of 1 month

series_description: Records maintained by OTTA's Compliance Officer relating to the training and compliance of proposed or established executive branch standards of ethical conduct and other etics regulationis; conflict of interest and other ethics-related statutes and Executive Orders; and any agency supplemental standards of ethical conduct and other agency ethics-related regulations and directives
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy when 3 years old or when superseded or obsolete, whichever is later. Duplicates: retain until obsolete, superseded or administrative value is lost. ; Electronic: Destroy when 3 years old or when superseded or obsolete, whichever is later. Duplicates: retain until obsolete, superseded or administrative value is lost.
